title: Canvas Items, Tags, Layers, Rendering, and Redraw · Runtime Lens: Render Evidence
summary: Redraw duplicates thousands of line items, solver marks cover walls, stale IDs are mutated, and hit testing scans every object. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the render evidence increment in tagged layered Canvas render plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Verify item counts, coordinates, tags, layer order, changed topology, and native screenshot or inspection evidence separately.
Choose full, regional, or item-diff redraw from measured complexity while preventing duplicate artifacts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example puzzle team handles case 1825 during a keyboard-only puzzle tournament. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile retained render items, semantic tags, layer order, and redraw cleanup; native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution; then replace pointer input with a single-action keyboard path. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Verify item counts, coordinates, tags, layer order, changed topology, and native screenshot or inspection evidence separately. A defensible solution applies Render Evidence, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to tagged layered Canvas render plan.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-canvas-rendering-tags-2
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-canvas-rendering-tags-2.
# Case 7417: a keyboard-only puzzle tournament.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def worked_maze_canvas_rendering_tags_2(expected_items: int = 126, actual_items: int = 126, semantic_tags: int = 5, duplicate_items: int = 0, layer_errors: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "gridworked2",
        "topology7417",
        "search2dekrow",
        "eventworked27417",
        "access7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if expected_items < 1 or actual_items < 0:
        return False, actual_items, "render-count"
    if actual_items != expected_items:
        return False, actual_items, "item-reconciliation"
    if semantic_tags < 4:
        return False, semantic_tags, "tag-contract"
    if duplicate_items > 0 or layer_errors > 0:
        return False, duplicate_items + layer_errors, "render-invariant"
    assert actual_items == expected_items
    return True, actual_items, "tagged-render-plan"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Verify item counts, coordinates, tags, layer order, changed topology, and native screenshot or inspection evidence separately.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Render Evidence model · Theory Render Evidence, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Render Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Render Evidence model · Theory Render Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 3635 during a classroom algorithm comparison. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile retained render items, semantic tags, layer order, and redraw cleanup; all graph claims need an independent witness; then change display scale and retain focus and control access. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Verify item counts, coordinates, tags, layer order, changed topology, and native screenshot or inspection evidence separately.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Render Evidence model · Theory Render Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: A screenshot can prove hidden item identity and cleanup.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: maze-render-evidence-otq0bf.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: maze-render-evidence-otq0bf(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_maze_render_evidence_otq0bf\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?expected_items < 1 or actual_items < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?actual_items != expected_items)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?semantic_tags < 4)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?duplicate_items > 0 or layer_errors > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
